title: Crepidium stenophyllum

The genus Crepidium is part of the larger subtribe Pleurothallidinae, along with other closely related species. It is also classified as part of the tribe Maxillarieae. intro: Crepidium stenophyllum is a species of orchid, specifically a type of terrestrial orchid, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepidium stenophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Crepidium stenophyllum is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
